_comment: Tolerances for granite_nar (NLE non-autoregressive editor).
Correctness regime:
- Reference: transformers 5.5.3, NLENARDecoder (modeling_nle.py), bf16
weights, attn_implementation='eager' (so is_causal=False yields a
truly bidirectional forward on layers that hard-assert flash-attn).
- Reference framework: transformers + trust_remote_code; eager attn.
- C++: bf16 weights (F32 norms, F32 biases, F16 conv kernels), F32
accumulators, CPU backend, threads=1.
- Mel: reference Whisper feature extractor (15-mel log-mel + 2-frame
stack); enc.mel.in tolerance reflects librosa-vs-host-FFT precision.
- KV cache: not used (single-pass bidirectional forward).
Dominant drift sources:
- Encoder Conformer blocks: standard bf16 reduction-order noise. The
last layer accumulates the bypass-induced residual plus 16 layers of
bf16 mul_mat noise. enc.block.15.out shows a localized drift band
around t=348-361 (a single Shaw block-local window boundary) on the
jfk sample, raising max_abs >> mean_abs. The mid-CTC-bypass dump
enc.block.7.out is tighter because it's BEFORE the bypass.
- enc.ctc_logits: 1024->348 linear acting on the high-drift final
hidden state propagates the same band into the char-CTC head.
- proj.qformer.out / proj.out: NLE Q-Former (no self-attention; 2
cross-attn+MLP layers). Drift comes from the encoder + bf16 cross
attention. The reference uses SDPA in BF16; we use mul_mat + soft_max
in F32 (slightly tighter precision but accumulated bf16 weight noise
dominates).
All numbers measured with `validate.py all --family granite_nar --variant
granite-speech-4.1-2b-nar` on a single jfk dump pair. Bands reflect 1.5x
observed; means use the 1.5x rule too. Floors at 1e-6.
The dec.* entries are intentionally absent: the NAR forward is shape-
dependent on the BPE-CTC initial hypothesis (n_text = 2n + 1 with
min 8), and tiny bf16 noise at the CTC head's argmax can flip enough
frames between blank and non-blank to produce a much longer (or shorter)
hypothesis. The reference and C++ end up with different n_text and
therefore different shapes. We sign off on the encoder + projector
tensor parity here and rely on the WER subset and the family-doc
Capability Validation table to gate end-to-end correctness.

enc.mel.in._comment: C++ MelFrontend vs HF WhisperFeatureExtractor. Observed 2.17e-5 max / 2.98e-7 mean from librosa-vs-host-FFT precision differences in the 400-sample STFT window. Widened 1.5x.

enc.input_linear.out._comment: bf16 mul_mat noise on the 160->1024 linear. Observed 1.55e-2 max / 1.83e-4 mean; widened 1.5x.

enc.block.7.out._comment: Layer 7 output BEFORE the self-conditioned CTC bypass injection. Observed 8e-2 / 1.20e-3; widened 1.5x.

enc.block.8.out._comment: Layer 8 output (the layer immediately after the bypass injection seeds the residual). Observed 2.55e-2 / 4.49e-4; widened 1.5x.

enc.block.15.out._comment: Last Conformer block. Observed 5.93 / 2.05e-2 — the localized band at the last Shaw window boundary dominates max_abs while mean is small. Same drift pattern as AR granite enc.block.15.out (max 8.9 in tests/tolerances/granite.json).

enc.ctc_logits._comment: Final char-CTC head (348 vocab) over enc.block.15.out. Observed 9.01 / 8.33e-2; widened 1.5x. Argmax is robust on most positions but the drift band shifts a few frames between blank and non-blank — the BPE pool absorbs most of that.

proj.qformer.out._comment: NLE simplified Q-Former (2 cross-attn + MLP layers, learned 3-query, no self-attn). Observed 2.70e-1 / 2.10e-2; widened 1.5x.

proj.out._comment: out_linear projection of the qformer output to the LLM hidden dim. Observed 1.24e-1 / 9.97e-3; widened 1.5x.

dec.flat_embeds._comment: Pre-multiplier flat inputs_embeds = cat(audio/embedding_multiplier, embed_tokens(text_with_eos_slots)). Drift comes from the projector (bf16 cross-attn + MLP) on the audio side. Observed 1.06e-2 / 6.03e-4; widened 1.5x.

dec.text_logits._comment: Raw lm_head logits over the text-portion slice (NLE bypasses GraniteForCausalLM's /logits_scaling divisor — see decoder.cpp). 40 bf16 LLM layers + bf16 lm_head accumulate substantial noise on magnitudes that themselves run O(100) at confident positions. Argmax is robust because the top1 margin is large; the transcript matches the reference. Observed 1.34e2 / 1.32e1; widened ~1.5x.
